text: One of the most important skills in programming is knowing how to fix errors, or bugs. Debugging requires a systematic approach. First, you need to identify where the bug is. Try to reproduce the problem consistently. Then, use debugging tools like breakpoints, which pause your code at a specific line, or step-through features to examine variable values. Another helpful strategy is logging: adding print statements to track what your program is doing at each stage. By following these steps, you can solve problems more efficiently and avoid frustration.
